birth. This is a vague theory. lt can at best help us when the
calculated lagna is at the beginning or at the end of a sign.
VII. There is still another view. Take the navamsa sign of
the lagna. It has 200 minutes, duration. Find the dvadasamsa
of that navamsa sign. Each dvadasamsa has duration of 16
minutes and 40 seconds. Count the resultant dvadasamsa
from the navamsa lagna. If it falls in an odd sign like Mesha,
Vrishabha etc., the native is a male.
Each dvadasamsa of the navamsa lagna is of 16' .40" dura
tion. When the lagna is 25 deg.46' of Pisces, the navamsa
lagna is Kumbha. In the eighth navamsa 146 minutes are
over. This gives the ninth dvadasamsa. It is Tula, an odd sign.
This agrees with the sex of the native. But if the ayanamsa
value given by Lahiri is not accepted, the result can be differ
ent. This method can fix the lagna and not the time of birth.
